message:
fix(bundle): Add atomic writes to mergeJSONFile to prevent corruption

Replace direct os.WriteFile with atomic write pattern (temp + fsync + rename)
in mergeJSONFile function to prevent file corruption during JSON merges.

Why atomic writes matter:
- Direct writes can leave files in corrupted state if process crashes or
  system loses power mid-write
- The temp file + fsync + rename pattern ensures either the old complete
  file exists or the new complete file exists, never a partial file
- fsync forces data to disk before rename, preventing data loss from
  OS write caching
- Rename is atomic on POSIX systems, making the final swap instantaneous

Implementation:
1. Write merged JSON to temporary file (dstPath + ".tmp")
2. Call fsync to flush data to persistent storage
3. Close the file handle
4. Atomically rename temp file to destination path
5. Clean up temp file on any error in the pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
